title: Edviro: AI agents vs school energy waste (extended cut)
imageStyle: dark night scene with warm amber and acid-green energy glow, flowing power-line light trails, gold chrome accents, high contrast

scenes.text: Your school spends more on *energy than textbooks*
scenes.voiceSegment: Your school probably spends more on energy than on textbooks and computers combined.

scenes.label: of school energy is wasted
scenes.voiceSegment: U S districts burn nearly eight billion dollars a year on energy, and about thirty percent of it is just wasted.

scenes.leftText: One manager, hundreds of boilers, HVAC units and alarms
scenes.voiceSegment: Here's why it stays broken: one facilities manager, hundreds of boilers, HVAC units, and alarm dashboards. Nobody can watch it all. So a Y C startup called Edviro made AI agents that can.
The agents plug into the district's meters, controls, and utility data, and watch every building around the clock.

scenes.text: Catch the waste · simulate the fix · prove it on the bill
scenes.voiceSegment: When something starts wasting power, the agent catches it, simulates the fix with an energy world model, and then proves the savings on the actual utility bill.

scenes.label: saveable with low-cost fixes alone
scenes.voiceSegment: And the wild part: about ten percent of school energy can be saved with low-cost fixes alone. The money is just sitting there.

scenes.imagePrompt: large glowing abstract data visualization wall display in a modern school hallway at dusk, columns of light showing energy flows, a few silhouetted students looking at it
scenes.text: Beacon: public dashboards, so taxpayers see it too
scenes.voiceSegment: Edviro even puts public dashboards in front of the community, so students and taxpayers can see exactly where the energy money goes.
Less waste, more textbook money. Follow for daily AI news.
